prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) and most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for benzaldehyde.
Level of theory: Density-functional theory, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting; exact four-center integrals) as specified by tier `standard` and explicit functional/basis overrides, in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: single-point charge-partition and finite-difference N ± 1 analysis performed on the user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz geometry; no further optimization was done (DFT//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion states were run as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2) per default protocol.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`) calling PySCF for DFT SCF calculations; see CLI below.
Convergence & warnings: All three SCF calculations (N, N–1, N+1) converged; integrity status **ok**, trustworthy = true.  Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Headline result
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, index 2 in 0-based counting) with f⁺ = 0.39938965.
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.
(The dual descriptor highlights atom 3 as most susceptible to electrophilic attack and atom 1 as most nucleophilic.)
